author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXXI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a Title this Psalm hath, “Unto the end for the presses, on the fifth of the Sabbath, a Psalm to Asaph himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Into one title many mysteries are heaped together, still so that the lintel of the Psalm indicates the things within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As we have to speak of the presses, let no one expect that we shall speak of a vat, of a press, of olive baskets; because neither the Psalm hath this, and therefore it indicateth the greater mystery.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No such thing did ye hear in this when it was reading.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore take the presses for the mystery of the Church, which is now transacting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the presses we observe three things, pressure, and of the pressure two things, one to be laid up, the other to be thrown away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There takes place then in the press a treading, a crushing, a weight: and with these the oil strains out secretly into the vat, the lees run openly down the streets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look intently on this great spectacle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God ceaseth not to exhibit to us that which we may look upon with great joy, nor is the madness of the Circus to be compared with this spectacle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That belongeth to the lees, this to the oil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore ye hear the blasphemers babble impudently and say that distresses abound in Christian times; for ye know that they love to say this: and it is an old proverb, yet one that began from Christian times, “God gives no rain; count it to the Christians!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although it was those of old that said thus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But these now say also, “That God sends rain, count it to the Christians!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God sends no rain; we sow not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God sends rain; we reap not!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they wilfully make that an occasion of showing pride, which ought to make them more earnest in supplication, choosing rather to blaspheme than to pray.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When therefore they talk of such things, when they make such boasts, when they say these things, and say them in defiance, not with fear, but with loftiness, let them not disturb you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For suppose that pressures abound; be thou oil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the lees, black with the darkness of ignorance, be insolent; and let it, as though cast away in the streets, go gibing publicly: but do thou by thyself in thy heart, where He who seeth in secret will requite thee, strain off into the vat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: …To name some one thing about which even they murmur who make them: How great plunderings, they say, are there in our times, how great distresses of the innocent, how great robberies of other men’s goods!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus indeed thou takest notice of the lees, that other men’s goods are seized; to the oil thou givest no heed, that to the poor are given even men’s own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The old time had no such plunderers of other men’s goods: but the old time had no such givers of their own goods.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore also “on the fifth of the sabbath”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us go back to the first works of God, if perchance we may not there find somewhat in which we may also understand a mystery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the sabbath is the seventh day, on which “God rested from all His works,” intimating the great mystery of our future resting from all our works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First of the sabbath then is called that first day, which we also call the Lord’s day; second of the sabbath, the second day;…and the sabbath itself the seventh day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See ye therefore to whom this Psalm speaketh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it seems to me that it speaketh to the baptized.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on the fifth day God from the waters created animals: on the fifth day, that is, on the “fifth of the sabbath,” God said, “Let the waters bring forth creeping things of living souls.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See ye, therefore, ye in whom the waters have already brought forth creeping things of living souls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ye belong to the presses, and in you, whom the waters have brought forth, one thing is strained out, another is thrown away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are many that live not worthily of the baptism which they have received.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how many that are baptized have chosen rather to be filling the Circus than this Basilica!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many that are baptized are either making booths in the streets, or complaining that they are not made!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this Psalm, “For the presses,” and “on the fifth of the sabbath,” is sung “unto Asaph.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Asaph was a certain man called by this name, as Idithun, as Core, as other names that we find in the titles of the Psalms: yet the interpretation of this name intimates the mystery of a hidden truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Asaph, in fact, in Latin is interpreted “congregation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “For the presses, on the fifth of the sabbath,” it is sung “unto Asaph,” that is, for a distinguishing pressure, to the baptized, born again of water, the Psalm is sung to the Lord’s congregation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have read the title on the lintel, and have understood what it means by these “presses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now if you please let us see the very house of the composition, that is, the interior of the press.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us enter, look in, rejoice, fear, desire, avoid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all these things ye are to find in this inward house, that is, in the text of the Psalm itself, when we shall have begun to read, and, with the Lord’s help, to speak what He grants us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold yourselves, O Asaph, congregation of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Exult ye unto God our helper” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye who are gathered together to-day, ye are this day the congregation of the Lord, if indeed unto you the Psalm is sung, “Exult ye unto God our helper.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Others exult unto the Circus, ye unto God: others exult unto their deceiver, do ye exult unto your helper: others exult unto their god their belly, do ye exult unto your God your helper.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Jubilate unto the God of Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because ye also belong to Jacob: yea, ye are Jacob, the younger people to which the elder is servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Jubilate unto the God of Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatsoever ye cannot explain in words, ye do not therefore forbear exulting: what ye shall be able to explain, cry out: what ye cannot, jubilate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from the abundance of joys, he that cannot find words sufficient, useth to break out into jubilating; “Jubilate unto the God of Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Take the Psalm and give the tabret” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both “take,” and “give.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “take”? what, “give”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Take the Psalm, and give the tabret.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul saith in a certain place, reproving and grieving, that no one had communicated with him in the matter of giving and receiving.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “in the matter of giving and receiving,” but that which he hath openly set forth in another place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If we have sowed unto you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your carnal things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it is true that a tabret, which is made of hide, belongs to the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Psalm, therefore, is spiritual, the tabret, carnal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, people of God, congregation of God, “take ye the Psalm, and give the tabret:” take ye spiritual things, and give carnal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This also is what at that blessed Martyr’s table we exhorted you, that receiving spiritual things ye should give carnal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these which are built for the time, are needful for receiving the bodies either of the living or of the dead, but in time that is passing by.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall we after God’s judgment take up these buildings to Heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet without these we shall not be able to do at this time the things which belong to the possessing of Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore ye are eager in getting spiritual things, be ye devout in expending carnal things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Take the Psalm, and give the tabret:” take our voice, return your hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The pleasant psaltery, with the harp.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I remember that we once intimated to your charity the difference of psaltery and harp.…For heavenly is the preaching of the word of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if we wait for heavenly things, let us not be sluggish in working at earthly things; because, “the psaltery is pleasant,” but, “with the harp.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same is expressed in another way as above, “Take the Psalm, and give the tabret:” here for “Psalm,” is put “psaltery,” for “tabret,” “harp.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this, however, we are admonished, that to the preaching of God’s word we make answer by bodily works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sound the trumpet” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is, Loudly and boldly preach, be not affrighted! as the Prophet says in a certain place, “Cry out, and lift up as with a trumpet thy voice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sound the trumpet in the beginning of the month of the trumpet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was ordered, that in the beginning of the month there should be a sounding of the trumpet: and this even now the Jews do in bodily sort, after the spirit they understand it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the beginning of the month, is the new moon: the new moon, is the new life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the new moon?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If any, then, is in Christ, he is a new creature.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “sound the trumpet in the beginning of the month of the trumpet”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With all confidence preach ye the new life, fear not the noise of the old life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because it is a commandment for Israel, and a judgment for the God of Jacob” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where a commandment, there judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “They that have sinned in the Law, by the Law shall be judged.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the very Giver of the commandment, the Lord Christ, the Word made flesh, saith, “For judgment I am come into the world, that they that see not may see, and they that see may be made blind.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “That they that see not may see, they that see be made blind,” but that the lowly be exalted, the proud thrown down?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not they that see are to be made blind, but those who to themselves seem to see are to be convicted of blindness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is brought about in the mystery of the press, that they who see may not see, and they that see be made blind.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A testimony in Joseph He made that” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look you, brethren, what is it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Joseph is interpreted augmentation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye remember, ye know of Joseph sold into Egypt: Joseph sold into Egypt is Christ passing over to the Gentiles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There Joseph after tribulations was exalted, and here Christ, after the suffering of the Martyrs, was glorified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thenceforth to Joseph the Gentiles rather belong, and thenceforth augmentation; because, “Many are the children of her that was desolate, rather than of her that hath the husband.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He made it, till he should go out of the land of Egypt.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe that also here the “fifth of the sabbath” is signified: when Joseph went out from the land of Egypt, that is, the people multiplied through Joseph, he was caused to pass through the Red Sea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore then also the waters brought forth creeping things of living souls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No other thing was it that there in figure the passage of that people through the sea foreshowed, than the passing of the Faithful through Baptism; the apostle is witness: for “I would not have you ignorant, brethren,” he said, “that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea, and were all baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing else then the passing through the sea did signify, but the Sacrament of the baptized; nothing else the pursuing Egyptians, but the multitude of past sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see most evident mysteries.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Egyptians press, they urge; so then sins follow close, but no farther than to the water.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then dost thou fear, who hast not yet come, to come to the Baptism of Christ, to pass through the Red Sea?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “Red”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consecrated with the Blood of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why fearest thou to come?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The consciousness, perhaps, of some huge offences goads and tortures in thee thy mind, and says to thee that it is so great a thing thou hast committed, that thou mayest despair to have it remitted thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear lest there remain anything of thy sins, if there lived any one of the Egyptians!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when thou shalt have passed the Red Sea, when thou shalt have been led forth out of thine offences “with a mighty hand and with a strong arm,” thou wilt perceive mysteries that thou knowest not: since Joseph himself too, “when he came out of the land of Egypt, heard a language which he knew not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shalt hear a language which thou knowest not: which they that know now hear and recognise, bearing witness and knowing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shalt hear where thou oughtest to have thy heart: which just now when I said many understood and answered by acclamation, the rest stood mute, because they have not heard the language which they knew not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them hasten, then, let them pass over, let them learn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He turned away from burdens his back” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who “turned away from burdens his back,” but He that cried, “Come unto Me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In another manner this same thing is signified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What the pursuit of the Egyptians did, the same thing do the burdens of sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if thou shouldest say, From what burdens?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His hands in the basket did serve.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the basket are signified servile works; to cleanse, to manure, to carry earth, is done with a basket, such works are servile: because “every one that doeth sin, is the slave of sin;” and “if the Son shall have made you free, then will ye be free indeed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly also are the rejected things of the world counted as baskets, but even baskets did God fill with morsels; “Twelve baskets” did He fill with morsels; because “He chose the rejected things of this world to confound the things that were mighty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But also when with the basket Joseph did serve, he then carried earth, because he did make bricks.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His hands in the basket did serve.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In tribulation thou didst call on Me, and I delivered thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let each Christian conscience recognise itself, if it have devoutly passed the Red Sea, if with faith in believing and observing it hath heard a strange language which it knew not, let it recognise itself as having been heard in its tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that was a great tribulation, to be weighed down with loads of sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How does the conscience, lifted from the earth, rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, thou art baptized, thy conscience which was yesterday overladen, to-day rejoiceth thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast been heard in tribulation, remember thy tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Before thou camest to the water, what anxiety didst thou bear on thee! what fastings didst thou practise! what tribulations didst thou carry in thy heart! what inward, pious, devout prayers!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Slain are thine enemies; all thy sins are blotted out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In tribulation thou didst call upon Me, and I delivered thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I heard thee in the hidden part of the tempest.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not in a tempest of the sea, but in a tempest of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I proved thee in the water of contradiction.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly, brethren, truly, he that was heard in the hidden part of the tempest ought to be proved in the water of contradiction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he hath believed, when he hath been baptized, when he hath begun to go in the way of God, when he hath striven to be strained into the vat, and hath drawn himself out from the lees that run in the street, he will have many disturbers, many insulters, many detractors, many discouragers, many that even threaten where they can, that deter, that depress.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is all the “water of contradiction.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I suppose there are some here to-day, for instance, I think it likely there are some here whom their friends wished to hurry away to the circus, and to I know not what triflings of this day’s festivity: perchance they have brought those persons with them to church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whether they have brought those with them, or whether they have by them not permitted themselves to be led away to the circus, in the “water of contradiction” have they been tried.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not then be ashamed to proclaim what thou knowest, to defend even among blasphemers what thou hast believed.…However much the bad that are aliens may rage, O that our own bad people would not help them!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye recollect what was said of Christ, that He was thus born for “the fall of many, and the rising again of many, and for a sign to be spoken against.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know, we see: the sign of the Cross has been set up, and it has been spoken against.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There has been speaking against the glory of the Cross: but there was a title over the Cross which was not to be corrupted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is a title in the Psalm, “For the inscription of the title, corrupt thou not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was a sign to be spoken against: for the Jews said, “Make it not, King of the Jews, but make it, that He said I am the King of the Jews.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Conquered was the contradiction; it was answered, “What I have written, I have written.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All this, from the beginning of the Psalm up to this verse, we have heard of the oil of the press.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What remains is rather for grief and warning: for it belongs to the lees of the press, even to the end; perchance also not without a meaning in the interposition of the “Diapsalma.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even this too is profitable to hear, that he who sees himself already of the oil may rejoice; he that is in danger of running among the lees may beware.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To both give heed, choose the one, fear the other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear, O My people, and I will speak, and will bear witness unto thee”(ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not to a strange people, not to a people that belongs not to the press: “Judge ye,” He saith, “between Me and My vineyard.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Israel, if thou shalt have heard Me, there shall not be in thee any new god” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A “new god” is one made for the time: but our God is not new, but from eternity to eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And our Christ is new, perchance, as Man, but eternal God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what before the beginning?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And truly, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And our Christ Himself is the Word made flesh, that He might dwell in us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far be it, then, that there should be in any one a new god.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A new god is either a stone or a phantom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is not, saith one, a stone; I have a silver and a gold one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly did he choose to name the very costly things, who said, “The idols of the nations are silver and gold.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great are they, because they are of gold and silver; costly they are, shining they are; but yet, “Eyes they have, and see not!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: New are these gods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What newer than a god out of a workshop?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, though those now old ones spiders’ webs have covered over, they that are not eternal are new.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So much for the Pagans.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if there be error in thee, Thou wilt not worship a strange god.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou think not of a false god, thou wilt not worship a manufactured god: for “there will not” be in thee any strange god.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why wouldest thou adore what is not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I am the Lord thy God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “I am I that Am,” and indeed “I Am” He saith, I that Am, over every creature: yet to thee what good have I afforded in time?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who brought thee out of the land of Egypt.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not to that people alone is it said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we all were brought out of the land of Egypt, we have all passed through the Red Sea; our enemies pursuing us have perished in the water.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not be ungrateful to our God; let us not forget God that abideth, and fabricate in ourselves a new god.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I, who led thee out of the land of Egypt,” saith God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Open wide thy mouth, and I will fill it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou sufferest straitness in thyself because of the new god set up in thy heart; break the vain image, cast down from thy conscience the feigned idol: “open wide thy mouth,” in confessing, in loving: “and I will fill it,” because with me is the fountain of life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My people obeyed not My voice” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He would not speak these things except to His own people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “we know that whatsoever things the Law saith, it saith to them that are in the Law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Israel did not listen to Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Israel to Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O ungrateful soul!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through Me the soul, by Me the soul called, by Me brought back to hope, by Me washed from sins!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Israel did not listen to Me!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are baptized and pass through the Red Sea: but on the way they murmur, gainsay, complain, are stirred with seditions, ungrateful to Him who delivered them from pursuing enemies, who leads through the dry land, through the desert, yet with food and drink, with light by night and shade by day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I let them go according to the affections of their heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the press: the orifices are open, the lees run.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I let them go,” not according to the healthfulness of My commands; but, according to the affections of their heart: I gave them up to themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle also saith, “God gave them up to the desires of their own hearts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I let them go according to the affection of their heart, they shall go in their own affections.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is what ye shudder at, if at least ye are straining out into the hidden vats of the Lord, if at least ye have conceived a hearty love for His storehouses, there is what ye shudder at.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some stand up for the circus, some for the amphitheatre, some for the booths in the streets some for the theatres, some for this, some for that, some finally for their “new gods;” “they shall go in their own affections.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If My people would have heard Me, if Israel would have walked in My ways” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For perchance that Israel saith, Behold I sin, it is manifest, I go after the affections of my own heart: but what can I do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil doth this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Demons do this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the devil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the demons?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly thine enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Unto nothing all their enemies I would have brought down; and on them that oppress them I would have sent forth My hand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now what have they to do to complain of enemies?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Themselves are become the worse enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of enemies ye complain, yourselves, what are ye?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The enemies of God have lied unto Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou renounce?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he returns to what he renounced.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fact, what things dost thou renounce, except bad deeds, diabolical deeds, deeds to be condemned of God, thefts, plunderings, perjuries, manslayings, adulteries, sacrileges, abominable rites, curious arts.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore all those works “shall not possess the kingdom of God” (yea not the works, but “they that do such things;” for such works there shall be none in the fire: for they shall not, while burning in that fire, be committing theft or adultery; but “they that do such things shall not possess the kingdom of God”); they shall not therefore be on the right hand, with those to whom it shall be said, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom:” because, “they that do such things shall not possess the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore on the right they shall not be, there remaineth not but that they must be on the left.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To those on the left what shall He say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Go ye into eternal fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, “their time shall be for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Explain to us, then, saith one, how those that build wood, hay, stubble, on the foundation, do not perish, but “are saved, yet so as by fire”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An obscure question indeed that, but as I am able I tell you briefly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, there are men altogether despisers of this world, to whom nothing is pleasant that flows in the course of time, they cling not by love to any earthly works, holy, chaste, continent, just, perchance even selling all their goods and distributing to the poor, or “possessing as though they possessed not, and using this world as though not using it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are others who cling to things allowed to infirmity with a degree of affection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He robs not another of his estate, but so loves his own, that if he loses it he will be disturbed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He does not covet another’s wife, but so clings to his own, so cohabits with his own, as not therein to keep the measure prescribed in the laws, for the sake of begetting children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He does not take away other men’s things, but reclaims his own, and has a law-suit with his brother.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to such it is said, “Now indeed there is altogether a fault among you, because ye have law-suits with each other.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But these very suits he orders to be tried in the Church, not to be dragged into court, yet he says they are faults.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a Christian contends for earthly things more than becomes one to whom the kingdom of Heaven is promised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not the whole of his heart doth he raise upward, but some part of it he draggeth on the earth.…Therefore if thou lovest thy possession, yet dost not for its sake commit violence, dost not for its sake bear false witness, dost not for its sake commit manslaughter, dost not for its sake swear falsely, dost not for its sake deny Christ: in that thou wilt not for its sake do these things, thou hast Christ for a foundation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet because thou lovest it, and art saddened if thou losest it, upon the foundation thou hast placed, not gold, or silver, or precious stones, but wood, hay, stubble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Saved therefore thou wilt be, when that begins to burn which thou hast built, yet so as by fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For let no one on this foundation building adulteries, blasphemies, sacrileges, idolatries, perjuries, think he shall be “saved through fire,” as though they were the “wood, hay, stubble:” but he that buildeth the love of earthly things on the foundation of the kingdom of Heaven, that is upon Christ, his love of temporal things shall be burned, and himself shall be saved through the right foundation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 21. …“And He fed them of the fat of wheat, and from the rock with honey He satisfied them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the wilderness from the rock He brought forth water, not honey.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Honey” is wisdom, holding the first place for sweetness among the viands of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many enemies of the Lord, then, that lie unto the Lord, are fed not only of the fat of wheat, but also from the rock with honey, from the wisdom of Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many are delighted with His word, and with the knowledge of His sacraments, with the unfolding of His parables, how many are delighted, how many applaud with clamour!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this honey is not from any chance person, but “from the rock.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “the Rock was Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How many, then, are satisfied with that honey, cry out, and say, It is sweet; say, Nothing better, nothing sweeter could be thought or said! and yet the enemies of the Lord have lied unto Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I like not to dwell any more on matters of grief; although the Psalm endeth in terror to this purpose, yet from the end of it, I pray you, let us return to the heading: “Exult unto God our Helper.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Turned unto God.
